description:
Recto: Account or a receipt of goods. Indian spikenard is mentioned in line 1 (سادج/ساذج; this can also mean "simple") and in line 2 a "raw" (خام) good of some sort is mentioned but is mostly lost to damage. Also reference to a paste (maʿjūn) and the instruction "let it be ground" (yudaqq). MCD/ASE

On verso there are some lines from Megillat Esther.
description_he: פנים: חשבון או קבלת סחורות, מזכיר נרד הודי ו'חי' (גלם) ו'מעג'ון' (משחה) ו'יודק' (יכתש); גב: שורות ממגילת אסתר
